title: EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE - 211info
text: DESCHUTES COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rapid identification, support, assessment and treatment for teenagers and young adults who are experiencing the early signs of psychosis. Services include education about psychosis; medication along with medication monitoring; individual and group counseling; multi-family groups; occupational therapy; family education and support; vocational training and life-skills coaching; 24/7 crisis services, and connections to community resources. 1340 NW Wall Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 12-30 who have had a first episode of psychosis within the last 12 months or who are experiencing early at-risk symptoms for psychosis and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 322-7576 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 322-7576 Tara Hare Referrals (541) 213-6851 Crisis Hotline (541) 322-7500 ext 9 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for intake FEES: Accepts OHP and private insurances. Sliding scale fees available for uninsured patients. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable. Income information if applying for sliding scale fees. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Crook, Deschutes, and Jefferson Counties Services: Early Intervention for Mental Illness Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY FACILITIES/CENTERS - THE DROP REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - INTELLECTUAL AND DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. DESCHUTES COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH
